samples.xml_description:
<module id="multi_pipe_4bit">
    <description>Design a 4-bit unsigned number pipeline multiplier with two levels of registers to store intermediate values and control the multiplication process.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal used for synchronous operation.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal. Defined as 0 for chip reset and 1 for reset signal inactive.</input>
        <input id="mul_a" width_description="[3:0]">Input signal representing the multiplicand with a data width of "size" bits.</input>
        <input id="mul_b" width_description="[3:0]">Input signal representing the multiplier with a data width of "size" bits.</input>
        <output id="mul_out" width_description="[7:0]">Product output signal with a data width of 2*size bits.</output>
    </ports>
    
    <parameter_description>
        <constant id="size">Number of bits in the multiplicand and multiplier.</constant>
    </parameter_description>
    
    <implementation>
        // Extend inputs with zeros at MSB positions
        wire [size-1:0] ext_mul_a;
        wire [size-1:0] ext_mul_b;
        
        assign ext_mul_a = {size{1'b0}} | mul_a;
        assign ext_mul_b = {size{1'b0}} | mul_b;
        
        // Generate partial products based on multiplier bits
        genvar i;
        generate
            for(i = 0; i < size; i = i + 1) begin : gen_partial_products
                wire [size-1:0] pp;
                
                assign pp = (ext_mul_b[i]) ? (ext_mul_a << i) : 'd0;
            end
        endgenerate
        
        // Registers to store intermediate sums
        reg [size-1:0] regs[0:size-1];
        
        integer j;
        
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if(!rst_n) begin
                for(j = 0; j < size; j = j + 1) begin
                    regs[j] <= 0;
                end
            end else begin
                for(j = 0; j < size; j = j + 1) begin
                    regs[j] <= regs[j] + gen_partial_products[j].pp;
                end
            end
        end
        
        // Register to store final product
        reg [2*size-1:0] prod_reg;
        
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if(!rst_n) begin
                prod_reg <= 0;
            end else begin
                prod_reg <= regs[0] + regs[1] + regs[2] + regs[3];
            end
        end
        
        // Output the final product
        assign mul_out = prod_reg;
    </implementation>
endmodule
